codeReview.summary: The fix still does not exercise the real SwiftUI entrypoints or enforce strict same-anchor ordered parity, so the original cross-flow and Session Detail blockers remain unresolved.

issues.description: These tests still only validate SettingsManager, DataStore.fetchConversation, and stable-ID helpers. Despite the ViewInspector import, they never instantiate SessionDetailView, inspect the rendered hierarchy, or present ContextPackSheet, so VAL-CTXDETAIL-001/002/003/006/007/008/010 remain model-level rather than contract-grade view-surface coverage.

The same-anchor tests still call ContextPackService.assemble through assembleAnchored/assembleUnanchored instead of driving Dashboard → SessionDetail → ContextPackSheet, and the assertions remain too weak: they check count/presence and normalized concatenated bodies but never exact ordered sessionIds equality between entrypoints. Same-anchor and envelope-only regressions can still slip through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The context-pack-ui-worker procedure already says cross-flow checks must drive a real Dashboard → SessionDetail → ContextPackSheet harness and must not fall back to service-only helpers, but the current fix still does exactly that. A concrete harness example or stronger anti-pattern checklist in the skill docs would make the contract harder to bypass.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests.swift:141-201 and AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackCrossFlowTests.swift:199-445 only exercise DataStore/service helpers; no DashboardView, SessionDetailView, or ContextPackSheet instantiation appears in the test files.

summary: The fix still does not exercise the real SwiftUI entrypoints or enforce strict same-anchor ordered parity, so the original cross-flow and Session Detail blockers remain unresolved.
